articles.title: SpaceX said to plan public IPO filing as soon as Wednesday - Fortune
articles.description: Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company aims to kick off formal marketing as soon as June 4, price its IPO as early as June 11 and list on June 12

articles.title: ‘Billionaires versus billionaires’: Inside the bizarre scenes of the OpenAI trial - NBC News
articles.description: The three-week OpenAI trial had numerous markings of its subjects’ wealth, from an inflatable Elon Musk doll to fancy butt cushions.

articles.title: Trump Fed nominees oppose terms of keeping Jay Powell as temporary chair - Financial Times
articles.description: The central bank said the incumbent would remain chair pro tempore until Kevin Warsh is sworn in as early as next week

articles.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S. - CBS News
articles.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

articles.description: The fund’s latest 13F filing was submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.

articles.title: Berkshire Boosted Stake in Alphabet in First Quarter, Bought Delta Air, Sold Visa, Mastercard - Barron's
articles.description: Berkshire Hathaway boosted its stake in Alphabet to nearly 58 million shares on March 31 from almost 18 million shares at year-end.

articles.title: US federal watchdog sues Chick-fil-A operator for religious discrimination - BBC
articles.description: A franchisee of the fast-food restaurant is accused of refusing an employee time off to observe the Sabbath, which is on Saturday in her Christian church.

articles.title: The Global Bond Rout Catches Up With Wall Street’s Risk Rally - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Wall Street’s hard-charging risk rally just ran into the bond market.

articles.title: Global Bond Selloff Worsens as Rising Oil Prices Spook Investors - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Government bond markets tumbled around the world, sending yields surging from Japan to the US on intensifying fears that the war-driven price shock will force central banks to raise interest rates to contain the impact.

articles.title: AP finishes US restructuring with round of layoffs, part of strategic pivot from print journalism - AP News
articles.description: The Associated Press implemented a round of layoffs Friday of U.S.-based journalists. The layoffs finish a restructuring aimed at turning the news organization’s focus away from print journalism and newspapers to visual journalism and other revenue sources. T…

articles.title: Trump’s Stock Trades Come Under Fire—Eric Trump Insists They’re Fine - Forbes
articles.description: A new financial disclosure shows Trump has traded millions of dollars worth of stocks in companies that include Nvidia, Palantir, Meta, Disney and more.

articles.title: Original Tommy's marks 80th anniversary with 80-cent chili cheeseburgers - ABC7 Los Angeles
articles.description: L.A.'s iconic Original Tommy's burger chain kicked off a celebration of its 80th anniversary by offering 80-cent chili cheeseburgers in a one-day-only deal.

articles.title: Residents Fume as Fleet of Empty Self-Driving Cars Circle Their Homes for Hours - The Daily Beast
articles.description: Driverless cars are driving locals crazy.

articles.title: Ford City Mall To Close In June - Block Club Chicago
articles.description: Shoppers have until noon June 22 to visit the mall, which opened in 1965 and has been deteriorating for years. Sixteen tenants remain, including the city’s last JCPenney.

articles.title: Sam Sianis, Steward Of Chicago’s Legendary Billy Goat Tavern, Dies At 91 - Block Club Chicago
articles.description: Sianis spent decades leading and preserving one of Chicago’s most iconic taverns, known for its burgers, newspaper lore and Cubs curse history.
